title: eriogonum howellianum
intro: Eriogonum howellianum, also known as Howell's buckwheat, is a shrubby perennial plant that belongs to the Polygonaceae family, native to California and Oregon in the United States. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Native American medicine, to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Eriogonum howellianum include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Eriogonum howellianum can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
